summary: Scanned 42 files. Found 3 issues: 1 high severity (hardcoded secret), 1 medium (path traversal), 1 low (debug logging). No critical findings.

findings.title: Hardcoded secret in source
findings.message: A string that looks like an API key is assigned to a variable. Secrets should be loaded from environment variables or a secrets manager.
findings.remediation: Move the secret to an environment variable and load it with `os.environ.get('API_KEY')`.

findings.title: Potential path traversal
findings.message: User input is passed to `open()` without sanitization. An attacker could read arbitrary files by passing `../../etc/passwd` as the template path.
findings.remediation: Validate that the resolved path is within the expected directory using `Path.resolve()` and checking it starts with the root.

findings.title: Debug logging in production code
findings.message: A `print()` statement with potentially sensitive context data. Consider using the `logging` module with appropriate levels.
